PROOF OF SERVICE OF REPLY FILED BY DEFENDANT PARSONS CORPORATION A CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT PARSONS CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR A STAY OF THE PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. GIVEN THE MANNER IN WHICH THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO, IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION AND PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE WITHIN THE MEANING OF ARMENDARIZ. IT ALSO IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE WITHIN THE MEANING OF MERCURO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 96 CAL. APP. 4TH 167; INGLE V. CIRCUIT CITY INC. (9TH CIR. 2003) 328 F. 3D 1165. THE AGREEMENT LACKS MUTUALITY IN REQUIRING EMPLOYEE CLAIMS TO BE SUBMITTED TO ARBITRATION WHILE PERSERVING THE RIGHT OF THE EMPLOYER TO SEEK RELIEF IN COURT ON THE EMPLOYER'S LIKELY CLAIMS. THE AGREEMENT ALSO AUTHORIZES THE EMPLOYER TO UNILATERALLY AMEND OR TERMINATE THE AGREEMENT. THE LIMITATIONS ON DISCOVERY ARE PROBLEMATIC. MARTINEZ V. MASTER PROTECTION CORP.; (2004) 118 CAL. APP. 4TH 107, 119. TAKEN TOGETHER, UNCONSCIONABILITY PERMEATES THE AGREEMENT AND SEVERANCE OF PROVISIONS IS NOT APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Dec-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Parsons Corporation's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; And Form Interrogatories ' Employment Law, Set One, is granted in part and denied in part.

The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory Number 2. The response is adequate. As there may be follow-up answers, they must be sought through follow-up discovery.

The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Numbers 15, 16, and 17, and Form Interrogatories 210.2, 210.3, and 213.1. Plaintiff's responses are inadequate as the rough amounts must be stated even though they may be proven by expert testimony just as damages must be stated where a statement of damages is requested.

Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Ptlf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES -ATTY. ALAN COHEN, TEL. NO. 984-1943 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF JACKIE CRIVINAR;
ATTY. CAROLYN B. HALL OF BINGHAM MCCUTCHEN, TEL. NO. 393-2610 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING- THE COURTS ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS :Plaintiff Jackie Crivinar's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Numbers 34 and 49. The supplemental response to Number 34, while it was not set forth in the opposition, is inadequate; and the qualifications made in the response have no merit. The response and supplemental response are inadequate. The objections to Numbers 34 and 49 are overruled and ordered stricken, except for privilege. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity.
The motion is granted as to Number 48. The objections are overruled and ordered stricken, except for privilege. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity. The limitation of the response to a section of the corporation is overruled.
Further responses and production shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED =(EAH/612)

Defendant Parsons Corp.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For A Stay Of The Proceedings (Transferred From Dept. 301 Per Judge Busch'S Recusal)
Off Calendar Oct-31-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PARSONS CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR A STAY OF THE PROCEEDINGS.
APPEARANCES: Alan F. Cohen, Esq. of Law Offices of Alan F. Cohen 984-1943 for pltf & opposing party; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. GIVEN THE MANNER IN WHICH THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO, IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION AND PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE WITHIN THE MEANING OF ARMENDARIZ. IT ALSO IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE WITHIN THE MEANING OF MERCURO V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 96 CAL. APP. 4TH 167; INGLE V. CIRCUIT CITY INC. (9TH CIR. 2003) 328 F. 3D 1165. THE AGREEMENT LACKS MUTUALITY IN REQUIRING EMPLOYEE CLAIMS TO BE SUBMITTED TO ARBITRATION WHILE PERSERVING THE RIGHT OF THE EMPLOYER TO SEEK RELIEF IN COURT ON THE EMPLOYER'S LIKELY CLAIMS. THE AGREEMENT ALSO AUTHORIZES THE EMPLOYER TO UNILATERALLY AMEND OR TERMINATE THE AGREEMENT. THE LIMITATIONS ON DISCOVERY ARE PROBLEMATIC. MARTINEZ V. MASTER PROTECTION CORP.; (2004) 118 CAL. APP. 4TH 107, 119. TAKEN TOGETHER, UNCONSCIONABILITY PERMEATES THE AGREEMENT AND SEVERANCE OF PROVISIONS IS NOT APPROPRIATE. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
